Name: Venusaur V
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Solar Typhoon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Weedle
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

Name: Kakuna
Flavor-Text: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

Name: Eldegoss V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: While young, it has six gorgeous tails. When it grows, several new tails are sprouted.

Name: Victini
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

Name: Incineroar V
Effects: Attach up to 2 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sizzlipede
Flavor-Text: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Carvanha
Flavor-Text: With its sturdy jaws and fangs, it can easily chomp wooden boats to splinters. It fights with Basculin over food.

Name: Sharpedo
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It drinks in seawater and jets it from its rear to propel itself. It’s very sensitive to the scent of blood.

Name: Wailord V
Effects: Attach up to 3 [W] Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 120 damage for each heads.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drednaw V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Drednaw VMAX
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir V
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir VMAX
Effects: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Hatenna
Flavor-Text: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures’ emotions. If you don’t have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

Name: Hattrem
Flavor-Text: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

Name: Hatterene
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.

Name: Galarian Cursola V
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alcremie V
Effects: Heal 30 damage from each of your Benched Pokémon.
If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alcremie VMAX
Effects: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a [P] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Machop
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its whole body is composed of muscles. Even though it’s the size of a human child, it can hurl 100 grown-ups.

Name: Machoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

Name: Machamp
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each [F] Pokémon in your discard pile.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

Name: Lucario V
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Beatdown Smash.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zygarde
Effects: Discard a [F] Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When it rubs the rocks on its neck against you, that’s proof of its love for you. However, the rocks are sharp, so the gesture is quite painful!

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike.
Flavor-Text: The rocks in its mane are sharper than a knife. Fragments that break off are treasured as good luck charms.

Name: Rolycoly
Flavor-Text: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

Name: Grapploct V
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ekans
Flavor-Text: The eggs of bird Pokémon are its favorite food. It swallows eggs whole, so sometimes an egg gets stuck, and Ekans faints.

Name: Arbok
Flavor-Text: After stunning its opponents with the pattern on its stomach, it quickly wraps them up in its body and waits for them to stop moving.

Name: Galarian Zigzagoon
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its restlessness has it constantly running around. If it sees another Pokémon, it will purposely run into them in order to start a fight.

Name: Galarian Linoone
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It uses its long tongue to taunt opponents. Once the opposition is enraged, this Pokémon hurls itself at the opponent, tackling them forcefully.

Name: Galarian Obstagoon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may have your opponent discard cards from their hand until they have 4 cards in their hand.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Its voice is staggering in volume. Obstagoon has a tendency to take on a threatening posture and shout—this move is known as Obstruct.

Name: Absol
Flavor-Text: The elderly call it the disaster Pokémon and detest it, but interest in its power to predict disasters is on the rise.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

Name: Liepard
Flavor-Text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: If it locks eyes with you, watch out! Nothing and no one is safe from the reckless headbutts of this troublesome Pokémon.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
If you played Piers from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As halfhearted as this Pokémon’s kicks may seem, they pack enough power to shatter Conkeldurr’s concrete pillars.

Name: Trubbish
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its favorite places are unsanitary ones. If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

Name: Inkay
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar’s control.

Name: Nickit
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Duraludon V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swablu
Flavor-Text: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX.
Flavor-Text: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

Name: Bede
Description: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Full Heal
Description: Your Active Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Great Ball
Description: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Hop
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Hyper Potion
Description: Heal 120 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has at least 2 Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard 2 Energy from it.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Kabu
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Marnie
Description: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Milo
Description: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand, and draw 2 cards for each card you discarded in this way.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Piers
Description: Search your deck for an Energy card and a [D] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Pokémon Center Lady
Description: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Potion
Description: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.
Flavor-Text: Professor’s Research (Magnolia)

Name: Rotom Bike
Description: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. Your turn ends.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Rotom Phone
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and shuffle the other cards back into your deck. Then, put the card you chose on top of your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Sonia
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon or up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Suspicious Food Tin
Description: Heal 80 damage from 1 of your Pokémon that has at least 1 [P] Energy attached. If you healed any damage in this way, discard a [P] Energy from it.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Team Yell Grunt
Description: Put an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon into their hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Turffield Stadium
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for an Evolution [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Drednaw V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir V
Effects: If this Pokémon was healed during this turn, this attack does 80 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Cursola V
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Grapploct V
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Hop
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Charizard VMAX
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir VMAX
Effects: Heal 50 damage from this Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Kabu
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. If your Active Pokémon is your only Pokémon in play, draw 8 cards instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Piers
Description: Search your deck for an Energy card and a [D] Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Charizard V
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
